input.headline: Ireland to face Czech Republic with home final possible

raw.headline: Ireland to face Czech Republic with home final possible

winner: Why did the Irish leprechaun bring a map to the game against the Czechs? Because he heard there was a pot of gold at the end of the line...and a home final!

candidates.text: Why did the Irish leprechaun bring a map to the game against the Czechs? Because he heard there was a pot of gold at the end of the line...and a home final!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative twist on a classic joke, ties in the specific teams and the home final possibility with a pun on 'pot of gold' and 'end of the line'."}

candidates.text: Why did Ireland bring a map to the game against the Czech Republic? To help find out if they're playing at home or on foreign soil!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is clever and specific to the headline, using a pun on 'foreign soil' for a soccer game context. It's brief and somewhat surprising, though the humor is mild and could be more impactful."}

candidates.text: Why did Ireland bring a Czech refrigerator to the game? They wanted to keep their home final cool and refreshing!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat creative with the refrigerator reference, but the connection to a home final is weak and the humor feels forced."}

candidates.text: In a surprising twist, the Czech Republic will be hosting Ireland for an away final, with the possibility of Ireland returning home triumphant... or homesick.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on 'home final' with a clever play on 'homesick,' concise and specific to the headline."}

candidates.text: The potato famine was never really about the potatoes, was it?
